# mdpress - Complete LLM Reference

> Turn Markdown and AI output into branded PDFs

mdpress renders Markdown files to professionally formatted PDFs with branding support. This reference teaches language models how to author mdpress-compatible Markdown documents.

---

## Variable Syntax (Mustache Templating)

Variables enable data-driven document generation. This is a Pro feature requiring the --data flag and Pro license.

### Basic Interpolation

Syntax: {{ variable }}

Inserts the value of a variable. HTML entities are escaped by default.

Example:
  Template: Hello, {{ name }}!
  Data: { "name": "World" }
  Output: Hello, World!

GOOD:
  {{ client_name }}
  {{ invoice_number }}
  {{ date }}

BAD:
  {{client_name}}      <- works but less readable, prefer spaces
  {{ CLIENT_NAME }}    <- case must match data exactly
  {{ name | upper }}   <- filters not supported

### Nested Object Access

Syntax: {{ object.property }} or {{ object.nested.path }}

Access properties within nested objects using dot notation.

Example:
  Template: Client: {{ client.name }}, City: {{ client.address.city }}
  Data: { "client": { "name": "Acme", "address": { "city": "NYC" } } }
  Output: Client: Acme, City: NYC

GOOD:
  {{ client.name }}
  {{ client.address.city }}
  {{ company.contact.email }}

BAD:
  {{ client['name'] }}         <- bracket notation not supported
  {{ client?.name }}           <- optional chaining not supported

### Loops/Sections

Syntax: {{#items}}...{{/items}}

Iterate over arrays. Inside the loop, use {{ . }} for the current item or access properties directly.

Example with simple array:
  Template:
    Items:
    {{#items}}
    - {{ . }}
    {{/items}}
  Data: { "items": ["Apple", "Banana"] }
  Output:
    Items:
    - Apple
    - Banana

Example with object array:
  Template:
    {{#products}}
    - {{ name }}: ${{ price }}
    {{/products}}
  Data: { "products": [{ "name": "Widget", "price": "19.99" }] }
  Output:
    - Widget: $19.99

GOOD:
  {{#items}}
  - {{ description }}: ${{ amount }}
  {{/items}}

BAD:
  {{#items}}{{ . }}{{/items}}  <- works but hard to read
  {% for item in items %}      <- Jinja syntax not supported
  {{#items}}{{ items.name }}   <- inside loop, access properties directly

### Conditionals

Syntax: {{#condition}}...{{/condition}}

Renders content only if the value is truthy (true, non-empty string, non-empty array).

Example:
  Template: {{#premium}}Premium Member{{/premium}}
  Data: { "premium": true }
  Output: Premium Member

  Data: { "premium": false }
  Output: (empty)

GOOD:
  {{#show_notes}}
  ## Notes
  {{ notes }}
  {{/show_notes}}

BAD:
  {{#if show_notes}}     <- "if" is literal, not a keyword
  {{#show_notes == true}}  <- comparison operators not supported

### Inverted Conditionals

Syntax: {{^condition}}...{{/condition}}

Renders content only if the value is falsy (false, empty string, empty array, undefined).

Example:
  Template: {{^items}}No items found.{{/items}}
  Data: { "items": [] }
  Output: No items found.

GOOD:
  {{^paid}}
  **Payment pending**
  {{/paid}}

BAD:
  {{!paid}}              <- wrong syntax
  {{^paid == false}}     <- comparison not supported

### Unescaped Content

Syntax: {{{ variable }}} (triple braces)

Outputs content without HTML escaping. Use when your data contains intentional HTML/Markdown.

Example:
  Template: {{{ formatted_notes }}}
  Data: { "formatted_notes": "**Bold** and *italic*" }
  Output: **Bold** and *italic*

GOOD:
  {{{ html_content }}}
  {{{ markdown_snippet }}}

BAD:
  {{{ user_input }}}     <- security risk if untrusted

---

## Directive Reference

Directives control document layout and semantic structure. They use the {{@name}} syntax and must appear on their own line.

### Parser Rules

- Directive: starts with {{@ (e.g., {{@pagebreak}})
- Variable: starts with {{ without @ (e.g., {{ name }})
- Block directives have opening and closing tags: {{@name}}...{{/name}}
- Self-closing directives stand alone: {{@pagebreak}}

### Layout Directives

#### {{@pagebreak}}

Forces a page break at this location.

Type: Self-closing

GOOD:
  ## Chapter 1

  Content here...

  {{@pagebreak}}

  ## Chapter 2

  More content...

BAD:
  Content {{@pagebreak}} more content   <- must be on own line
  {{@ pagebreak }}                       <- no spaces inside tag
  {{@page-break}}                        <- wrong name

#### {{@columns}}...{{/columns}}

Renders enclosed content in a multi-column layout.

Type: Block

GOOD:
  {{@columns}}
  **Left Column**

  Content for the left side.

  ---

  **Right Column**

  Content for the right side.
  {{/columns}}

BAD:
  {{@columns}}Left{{/columns}}{{@columns}}Right{{/columns}}  <- use one block
  {{@columns cols=3}}                                          <- attributes not supported

#### {{@toc}}

Generates a table of contents based on document headings.

Type: Self-closing

GOOD:
  # Document Title

  {{@toc}}

  ## Introduction
  ...

  ## Methods
  ...

BAD:
  {{@toc depth=2}}       <- attributes not supported
  {{@tableofcontents}}   <- wrong name

### Semantic Directives

#### {{@note}}...{{/note}}

Renders content as a note callout box. Use for helpful tips or additional information.

Type: Block

GOOD:
  {{@note}}
  Remember to save your work frequently.
  {{/note}}

BAD:
  {{@note}}Inline note{{/note}}        <- should be on own lines
  > **Note:** Remember to save...       <- use directive instead

#### {{@warning}}...{{/warning}}

Renders content as a warning callout box. Use for cautions or important alerts.

Type: Block

GOOD:
  {{@warning}}
  This action cannot be undone. Please backup your data first.
  {{/warning}}

BAD:
  {{@warning}}Danger!{{/warning}}      <- should be on own lines
  **WARNING:** This action...           <- use directive instead

#### {{@highlight}}...{{/highlight}}

Renders content with visual emphasis/highlighting.

Type: Block

GOOD:
  {{@highlight}}
  Key takeaway: Always validate user input before processing.
  {{/highlight}}

BAD:
  The {{@highlight}}important{{/highlight}} part   <- must be block level

---

## Unsupported Patterns

These patterns will cause errors or unexpected output:

### Inline Directives
Directives cannot appear inline within text.

BAD:
  The total is {{@highlight}}$500{{/highlight}} due today.

GOOD:
  {{@highlight}}
  The total is $500 due today.
  {{/highlight}}

### Unknown Directives
Only use documented directives. Unknown directives cause parse errors.

BAD:
  {{@custom}}
  {{@sidebar}}
  {{@footer}}

### Raw CSS
CSS is not supported. Use Markdown and directives for formatting.

BAD:
  <style>
  h1 { color: red; }
  </style>

  <div style="color: blue">Text</div>

### Raw HTML (Limited)
Basic HTML may work but is not guaranteed. Prefer Markdown.

BAD:
  <table><tr><td>Cell</td></tr></table>

GOOD:
  | Header |
  |--------|
  | Cell   |

### Template Logic
Complex logic, filters, and macros are not supported.

BAD:
  {{ price | currency }}
  {{ name | uppercase }}
  {% if condition %}
  {% macro button() %}

---

## Worked Examples

### Example 1: Invoice

Template (invoice.md):
```
# Invoice

**Invoice Number:** {{ invoice_number }}
**Date:** {{ date }}

---

## Bill To

{{ client.name }}
{{ client.address.street }}
{{ client.address.city }}, {{ client.address.state }} {{ client.address.zip }}

---

## Line Items

| Description | Quantity | Unit Price | Total |
|-------------|----------|------------|-------|
{{#items}}
| {{ description }} | {{ quantity }} | ${{ unit_price }} | ${{ total }} |
{{/items}}

---

**Subtotal:** ${{ subtotal }}
**Tax ({{ tax_rate }}%):** ${{ tax_amount }}
**Total Due:** ${{ total_due }}

{{@note}}
Payment is due within {{ payment_terms }} days.
{{/note}}

{{#notes}}

---

## Notes

{{ . }}
{{/notes}}
```

Data (invoice.json):
```json
{
  "invoice_number": "INV-2024-001",
  "date": "2024-03-15",
  "client": {
    "name": "Acme Corporation",
    "address": {
      "street": "123 Business Ave",
      "city": "San Francisco",
      "state": "CA",
      "zip": "94102"
    }
  },
  "items": [
    { "description": "Consulting Services", "quantity": "10", "unit_price": "150.00", "total": "1500.00" },
    { "description": "Development Work", "quantity": "20", "unit_price": "125.00", "total": "2500.00" }
  ],
  "subtotal": "4000.00",
  "tax_rate": "8.5",
  "tax_amount": "340.00",
  "total_due": "4340.00",
  "payment_terms": "30",
  "notes": "Thank you for your business!"
}
```

Command:
```
mdpress render invoice.md --data invoice.json --template invoice
```

---

### Example 2: Technical Report

Template (report.md):
```
# {{ title }}

**Author:** {{ author }}
**Date:** {{ date }}
**Version:** {{ version }}

{{@toc}}

{{@pagebreak}}

## Executive Summary

{{ executive_summary }}

{{@highlight}}
{{ key_finding }}
{{/highlight}}

{{@pagebreak}}

## Introduction

{{ introduction }}

## Methodology

{{ methodology }}

{{#methodology_steps}}
1. {{ . }}
{{/methodology_steps}}

## Results

{{ results_overview }}

{{@warning}}
{{ limitations }}
{{/warning}}

### Data Analysis

{{#data_points}}
- **{{ label }}:** {{ value }} ({{ interpretation }})
{{/data_points}}

{{@pagebreak}}

## Conclusions

{{ conclusions }}

## Recommendations

{{#recommendations}}
- {{ . }}
{{/recommendations}}

{{@note}}
For questions about this report, contact {{ contact_email }}.
{{/note}}
```

Data (report.yaml):
```yaml
title: "Q1 2024 Performance Analysis"
author: "Data Analytics Team"
date: "2024-04-01"
version: "1.0"

executive_summary: |
  This report analyzes Q1 2024 performance metrics across all departments.
  Overall performance exceeded targets by 12%.

key_finding: "Revenue growth of 23% represents the strongest Q1 in company history."

introduction: |
  This quarterly analysis examines key performance indicators across
  sales, marketing, and operations departments.

methodology: "Data was collected from internal systems and validated against financial records."

methodology_steps:
  - "Extracted data from CRM and ERP systems"
  - "Normalized data formats across sources"
  - "Applied statistical analysis"
  - "Validated findings with department heads"

results_overview: "All departments met or exceeded their quarterly targets."

limitations: |
  Note: Marketing data from January 1-15 was estimated due to a system migration.

data_points:
  - label: "Sales Revenue"
    value: "$2.4M"
    interpretation: "+23% YoY"
  - label: "Customer Acquisition"
    value: "847"
    interpretation: "+15% vs target"
  - label: "Customer Retention"
    value: "94%"
    interpretation: "Above industry average"

conclusions: |
  Q1 2024 demonstrated strong performance across all measured dimensions.
  The sales team particularly excelled in enterprise account growth.

recommendations:
  - "Increase investment in enterprise sales team"
  - "Expand marketing automation capabilities"
  - "Implement customer success program"

contact_email: "analytics@company.com"
```

---

### Example 3: Agent-Generated Report

This example shows how an AI agent might generate an mdpress document.

Template (agent-report.md):
```
# {{ report_type }}: {{ subject }}

**Generated:** {{ timestamp }}
**Agent:** {{ agent_name }}
**Confidence:** {{ confidence_level }}

{{@toc}}

{{@pagebreak}}

## Summary

{{ summary }}

{{#high_priority}}
{{@warning}}
**High Priority Items Detected**

{{#items}}
- {{ . }}
{{/items}}
{{/high_priority}}

## Analysis

{{#sections}}
### {{ title }}

{{ content }}

{{#findings}}
{{@note}}
**Finding:** {{ description }}
**Impact:** {{ impact }}
**Recommendation:** {{ recommendation }}
{{/note}}
{{/findings}}

{{/sections}}

{{@pagebreak}}

## Appendix

### Data Sources

{{#data_sources}}
- {{ name }}: {{ description }}
{{/data_sources}}

### Methodology Notes

{{ methodology_notes }}

{{^reviewed}}
{{@warning}}
This report has not been reviewed by a human analyst.
{{/warning}}
{{/reviewed}}

---

*Report generated automatically by {{ agent_name }}*
```

Data (agent-report.json):
```json
{
  "report_type": "Security Audit",
  "subject": "Web Application Assessment",
  "timestamp": "2024-03-20T14:32:00Z",
  "agent_name": "SecurityBot v2.1",
  "confidence_level": "High (94%)",
  "summary": "Completed security assessment of the production web application. Identified 3 issues requiring attention, with 1 high-priority vulnerability.",
  "high_priority": {
    "items": [
      "SQL injection vulnerability in /api/search endpoint",
      "Exposed debug endpoint in production"
    ]
  },
  "sections": [
    {
      "title": "Authentication",
      "content": "Authentication mechanisms were tested for common vulnerabilities.",
      "findings": [
        {
          "description": "Session tokens use secure random generation",
          "impact": "Positive - reduces session hijacking risk",
          "recommendation": "No action required"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Input Validation",
      "content": "All user input endpoints were tested for injection vulnerabilities.",
      "findings": [
        {
          "description": "SQL injection in search endpoint",
          "impact": "Critical - potential data breach",
          "recommendation": "Implement parameterized queries immediately"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "data_sources": [
    { "name": "OWASP ZAP", "description": "Automated vulnerability scanner" },
    { "name": "Custom Scripts", "description": "Manual testing procedures" }
  ],
  "methodology_notes": "Testing followed OWASP Testing Guide v4.2 methodology.",
  "reviewed": false
}
```

---

## Agent Instructions

When generating mdpress-compatible Markdown:

### DO

1. **Use standard Markdown first** - Headers, lists, tables, bold, italic all work as expected
2. **Place directives on their own lines** - Never inline
3. **Use semantic directives appropriately** - {{@note}} for tips, {{@warning}} for alerts
4. **Structure data properly** - Flat keys for simple values, nested objects for related data
5. **Test variable names** - Ensure all {{ variables }} have corresponding data keys
6. **Include page breaks** - Use {{@pagebreak}} between major sections for longer documents

### DON'T

1. **Don't use inline directives** - `The {{@highlight}}total{{/highlight}}` will fail
2. **Don't invent directives** - Only use documented ones
3. **Don't use complex templating** - No filters, macros, or logic operators
4. **Don't include raw CSS** - Styling comes from templates
5. **Don't assume HTML works** - Stick to Markdown

### Variable Data Best Practices

1. **Use descriptive keys** - `invoice_number` not `num`
2. **Keep nesting shallow** - 2-3 levels maximum
3. **Use arrays for repetition** - Not numbered keys like `item1`, `item2`
4. **Provide all referenced variables** - Missing variables cause errors

### Template Structure

Recommended document structure:
```
# Title with {{ variable }}

Metadata block (date, author, etc.)

{{@toc}}            <- if document has multiple sections

{{@pagebreak}}      <- start content on fresh page

## Section 1

Content...

{{@pagebreak}}      <- between major sections

## Section 2

Content...
```

---

## MCP Tool Integration

<!-- TODO: MDP-65 - Add MCP tool documentation -->

MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool integration is planned for a future release. This will enable:

- Direct rendering from AI assistants
- Template discovery and selection
- Data validation before rendering
- Real-time preview capabilities

Check back for updates or see the project repository for current status.

---

## Quick Reference Card

| Syntax | Purpose | Type |
|--------|---------|------|
| `{{ var }}` | Variable interpolation | Variable |
| `{{ obj.key }}` | Nested access | Variable |
| `{{#arr}}...{{/arr}}` | Loop over array | Variable |
| `{{#bool}}...{{/bool}}` | Conditional | Variable |
| `{{^bool}}...{{/bool}}` | Inverted conditional | Variable |
| `{{{ var }}}` | Unescaped output | Variable |
| `{{@pagebreak}}` | Page break | Directive |
| `{{@toc}}` | Table of contents | Directive |
| `{{@columns}}...{{/columns}}` | Multi-column | Directive |
| `{{@note}}...{{/note}}` | Note callout | Directive |
| `{{@warning}}...{{/warning}}` | Warning callout | Directive |
| `{{@highlight}}...{{/highlight}}` | Emphasis block | Directive |

---

*This reference is designed for consumption by language models. For human documentation, see README.md.*
